meta.title: TechPulse
meta.tagline: Tech & AI news, hot takes, and who's saying what — from the builders you follow
meta.note: Built from your X Following timeline, filtered to tech/AI builders by profile. Click any story to see every take and what different people are saying.

stories.title: Fable 5 Is Fully Back — Rerouting Fears Were Overblown
stories.summary: After days of rate-limit and rerouting drama, Claude Fable 5 rolled back out across Vercel AI Gateway, GitHub Copilot, and Cursor — and builders across the timeline say it's coding as well as ever, with barely any Opus fallback in practice.

takes.name: Theo - t3.gg
takes.text:
I've been getting a TON done with Fable today and I'm not hitting rate limits. Wanted to share some tips on how I'm doing that

1. I only use Fable on "high" effort for now. xhigh is token hungry. max/extra is a furnace with worse outputs than lower options imo

2. I taught

takes.name: Riley Brown
takes.text:
omg... Fable 5 is back and it's so good

I asked Fable to create a game called: The race for Super Intelligence... 

After 4 prompts, and $173 worth of tokens Fable 5 created this game. (Prompt Below) https://t.co/mhBsY6vouo

takes.name: Layton Gott
takes.text:
There were SO MANY rumors about Fable 5 not being able to code anymore…

Yet it’s been able to do everything I’ve asked it to do so far without falling back to Opus 4.8.

takes.name: GitHub
takes.text: ➡️ @AnthropicAI's Claude Fable 5 is now re-enabled and generally available in GitHub Copilot.

takes.name: Tim Sweeney
takes.text: Thanks for keeping us safe Claude Fable 5! https://t.co/hN30sw1Nv4

takes.name: Vercel Developers
takes.text:
Claude Fable 5 is back on Vercel AI Gateway.

𝚖𝚘𝚍𝚎𝚕: '𝚊𝚗𝚝𝚑𝚛𝚘𝚙𝚒𝚌/𝚌𝚕𝚊𝚞𝚍𝚎-𝚏𝚊𝚋𝚕𝚎-𝟻'

https://t.co/zUGRskomGB

takes.name: dax
takes.text:
given how expensive (and slow) fable is we're trying to use it with the orchestrator + minion pattern (in this case GLM)

primary agent delegates all work and spawns them as background subagent

you can move on and continue to work on stuff and it'll keep this organized https://t.co/jcAn3bOC3f

takes.name: Mirko Monti
takes.text: Fable is back.... for some days https://t.co/NBobWGLTLl

takes.name: Steve Ruiz
takes.text: welcome back my friend https://t.co/WuJTkyPQzK

takes.name: Kevin Kern
takes.text: fable 5 is back in cursor too. https://t.co/b9984JSoc8

takes.name: mark
takes.text: I’m so glad to have Fable back https://t.co/7aieCzw5Vi

takes.name: Jan
takes.text: its me & fable against the world https://t.co/eeX3Vvvj42

takes.name: Tom Johnson
takes.text: fable looking at the code that opus has been writing over the last few weeks https://t.co/RVL2vtXs3Z

takes.name: James Perkins
takes.text: Fable 5 no thanks i’m busy with Uncharted 4.

takes.name: KapishDima
takes.text: Fable is currently making a demo video for Remocn. You're going to be surprised)

takes.name: Can Vardar
takes.text:
the plan is simple:

build the best todo app with fable 5 before it gets restricted again

takes.name: Marc Backes
takes.text: @theo What am I missing? Didn’t they say Fable is Opus for coding?

takes.name: Guillermo Rauch
takes.text:
We've been explaining AI Gateway as a C̶o̶n̶t̶e̶n̶t̶ Token Delivery Network. Like a CDN for AI models.

One great feature of CDNs is the ability to dynamically re-route or deny traffic without redeployment.

When Fable was suddenly retired, we worried about production workloads

takes.name: Claude
takes.text:
A conversation with Boris Cherny and Cat Wu on the path from Claude Code to Claude Tag, and how it spread from engineering to the rest of Anthropic.

Claude Fable 5 is now available in Claude Tag. https://t.co/8oNM5WaWzj

takes.name: Micky
takes.text: i love fable 5, it just feels so good

stories.title: Everyone's Building Their Own Agent Orchestration Playbook
stories.summary: From custom Claude Code skills to naming conventions for "context/loop/route engineering," builders are converging on shared patterns for running fleets of coding agents — orchestrators, subagents, and deterministic pipelines instead of one giant prompt.

takes.name: Matt Pocock
takes.text:
My new skill /wayfinder is letting me do stuff I've never considered trying

I'm planning an entire course with it every minute of the last four days

Closing in on 100 separate grilling/prototyping/research sessions

All contributing back to a central map that:

- Grows as I

takes.name: Kent C. Dodds 🏹
takes.text:
This is part of the premise of Kody 🐨

The goal is an intentional pipeline that converts your agent-manual processes into deterministic code as much as possible to improve performance and reliability as well as reduce costs.

takes.name: Can Vardar
takes.text: one of the best things about llms is that they understand git better than most developers

takes.name: dex
takes.text:
HUMANLAYER OFFICE HOURS @aiDotEngineer THURSDAY JULY 2

If you wanna hang with Dex and Kyle and chat about HumanLayer, we'll be live at Moscone West, come riff about the future of the software factory etc, bring your laptop, get set up, ask questions, etc!

10am - 12pm 
1pm - 3pm

takes.name: dax
takes.text: update: everyone is installing opencode2 bots on their machines and they're invading the team chat https://t.co/xIV3XZ8EYn

takes.name: Kevin Kern
takes.text:
prompt engineering - how to ask.
context engineering - what to include.
loop engineering - how to keep the agent going.
route engineering - which model or tool should handle each step.
factory engineering - how to turn all of it into a repeatable production system.

takes.name: Theo - t3.gg
takes.text: Gonna do a thread of Claude Code feedback for the team in hopes they fix some of my small annoyances

stories.title: The Cheap Model Race: Kimi K2.7 Lands in Copilot and Cursor
stories.summary: Moonshot's Kimi K2.7 went GA in GitHub Copilot as the first selectable open-weight model, then showed up in Cursor with strong evals against GLM 5.2 — while another Chinese lab (Unisound) undercut everyone at $0.18/M tokens claiming 72-75% on SWE-bench.

takes.name: Kimi Developers
takes.text:
Achieve your GOALLLLL with Kimi Code! 🚀

Kimi Code can now plan, execute, revise, and debug 24/7 until your goal is done.

Just give it a clear, specific, and achievable objective with  /goal  — then let it work step by step.

Try out  /goal  with Kimi K2.7-Code 👉 https://t.co/1GcBY82dXS

takes.name: GitHub
takes.text:
🆕 @Kimi_Moonshot's Kimi K2.7 Code is now generally available in GitHub Copilot.

This is the first open-weight model offered as a selectable option in the Copilot model picker. 🎉 

Early testing shows Kimi K2.7 is a lower-cost option with strong performance comparable to highly https://t.co/jHXIwEQU51

takes.name: Lee Robinson
takes.text:
You can now try Kimi K2.7 in Cursor!

Results from our evals ↓ Interesting to see the comparison with GLM 5.2. https://t.co/Y6GMj7uGay

takes.name: Layton Gott
takes.text:
$9 per million tokens was the normal price for a top coding model this year…

A new model just showed up charging ONLY $0.18.

Unisound U2. 

Another Chinese lab dropped it, claiming 72-75% on SWE-bench Verified.

For reference:

• Opus 4.8 scores 88.6 and costs $9
• Fable 5 https://t.co/SUdTZLoak0

stories.title: Vercel Doubles Down on AI Gateway and Private Service Bindings
stories.summary: Vercel shipped routing rules for AI Gateway (swap models via CLI, no code changes) plus new Service Bindings for private, low-latency connectivity between apps — part of a broader push to make Vercel the default control plane for agentic backends.

takes.name: Guillermo Rauch
takes.text:
Private connectivity between services is 🔑 for backends.

1️⃣ Register 𝚋𝚒𝚗𝚍𝚒𝚗𝚐𝚜 in 𝚟𝚎𝚛𝚌𝚎𝚕.𝚓𝚜𝚘𝚗 (1LOC)
2️⃣ Read it:

𝚗𝚎𝚠 𝚄𝚁𝙻("/𝚊𝚙𝚒", 𝚙𝚛𝚘𝚌𝚎𝚜𝚜.𝚎𝚗𝚟.𝙱𝙰𝙲𝙺𝙴𝙽𝙳_𝙸𝙽𝚃𝙴𝚁𝙽𝙰𝙻_𝚄𝚁𝙻)

Works for Node, Python, 𝙳𝚘𝚌𝚔𝚎𝚛𝚏𝚒𝚕𝚎… you name it

takes.name: Vercel Developers
takes.text:
AI Gateway now supports routing rules.

Migrate to new models by setting rules in the CLI, no code changes in your app or agent required. https://t.co/WyQd0omXEF

takes.name: Jared Palmer
takes.text: @rauchg @samlambert @vercel @PlanetScale @schniz Make a lil Wordpress Plugin to offload media to Vercel Blob

stories.title: AI Engineer SF Turns Into a Timeline Meetup
stories.summary: With the AI Engineer conference running in SF, T3's Theo and HumanLayer's Dex both threw open-door meetups at the show — a reminder that a big chunk of this feed is currently in the same building.

takes.name: Theo - t3.gg
takes.text:
Doing a little T3 Community meet and greet today at 2pm at AI Engineer

We’ll be at the OpenAI booth 🫡

takes.name: dex
takes.text:
Insane week at @aiDotEngineer !! @swyx and team are legends

If you’re around today, humanlayer is holding office hours on the second floor by the food stand, come say hi, bring your laptop, let’s ship! https://t.co/TTuvDE7ntU

stories.title: Higgsfield Bets Gemini Omni Flash Is the New AI Video Bar
stories.summary: Higgsfield ran a string of head-to-head comparisons (Fable 5 vs Sonnet 5 prompting Seedance 2.0, plus Gemini Omni Flash re-editing real footage into graded VFX) to argue Omni Flash is quietly the best video-editing model nobody's talking about — and made its MCP free for new users.

takes.name: Higgsfield AI 🧩
takes.text:
Fable 5 vs Sonnet 5.

Both videos were generated with Seedance 2.0 in 4K. https://t.co/D2lUx849qv

takes.name: Higgsfield AI 🧩
takes.text:
Gemini Omni Flash is exceptional at motion design generation, and almost nobody has noticed it edits motion design videos just as well.

We fed it one of our older videos and asked it to re-edit. https://t.co/KP87iLs5VG

takes.name: Higgsfield AI 🧩
takes.text:
Gemini Omni Flash turns real footage into finished VFX.

Shoot on your phone, describe the effect, and the shot comes back graded. https://t.co/oFAY2l4kAK

stories.title: Chollet: AI Is Converging on Symbolic World Modeling
stories.summary: François Chollet laid out a thesis that most AI progress is quietly morphing into 'LRM-guided harnesses that manipulate symbolic programs' — deep learning-guided program synthesis he calls inevitable, with ARC-AGI-3 leaderboard results already backing it up.

takes.name: François Chollet
takes.text: Eventually, much of AI will converge towards intuition-guided symbolic world modeling, i.e. deep learning-guided program synthesis. It is inevitable. Symbolic modeling lets a system construct a compact, reusable, highly generalizable mental model of a problem space using minimal

takes.name: François Chollet
takes.text: Even right now, many workflows are morphing into LRM-guided harnessess that manipulate symbolic programs. Which is a crude, but currently-accessible form of symbolic learning.

takes.name: François Chollet
takes.text: Unsurprisingly, all of the strong contenders on ARC-AGI-3 so far use this type of approach.

stories.title: Together AI Raises $800M at $8.3B, Doubles Down on Open Source
stories.summary: Together AI closed an $800M Series C at an $8.3B valuation, with DevRel lead Hassan Nutlope saying the company is doubling down on open source AI infrastructure.

takes.name: Hassan
takes.text:
Excited to share that we raised an $800M series C at an $8.3B valuation!

We're doubling down on open source AI 🫡 https://t.co/jR00fGsHhn

stories.title: Remocn Ships a Shader Toolkit for Remotion Video
stories.summary: Kapish Dima launched Remocn Shaders, a one-command install for Paper Shaders inside Remotion, aimed at making stunning video backgrounds and transitions trivial to add to programmatic video projects.

takes.name: KapishDima
KapishDima
takes.text: Thanks, @jalcowastaken, for sponsoring Remocn ❤️ https://t.co/bwiEDRoFw3

stories.title: 21 Years of Web Design, One Screenshot at a Time
stories.summary: Web Design Museum's side-by-side of The Pirate Bay's homepage in 2005 vs. 2026 (and a look back at Google's Linux-era search page from 2000) went viral as a reminder of just how far web design has come.

takes.name: Web Design Museum
takes.text:
21 years of evolution of web design trends.  

The Pirate Bay homepage in 2005
vs.
The Pirate Bay homepage in 2026

#WebDesignHistory https://t.co/vM7bzu7mLB

takes.name: Web Design Museum
takes.text:
Google Search Linux in 2000

https://t.co/JbXPsOOzhe

hotTakes.name: Riley Brown
hotTakes.text: The goal is not burning tokens, the goal is getting shit done. It just so happens best way to do that is to burn tokens.

hotTakes.name: Steve Ruiz
hotTakes.text: IMO the best open source applications look like they were designed by absolute morlocks. Ugly patchy functional labors of love. Remember that software does not want to exist

hotTakes.text:
having my own personal agent for work is quite nice.

is this what it’s like to be a ceo and have an executive assistant?

hotTakes.text: how tf is an llm making typos https://t.co/G2wQJg3juT

hotTakes.name: Can Vardar
hotTakes.text:
i haven’t seen anyone get hired from linkedin in a long time

it feels more like a place to announce you got hired than a place to actually get hired

hotTakes.name: Mirko Monti
hotTakes.text:
That’s a great interview by @PLTRs_Palantir 

For national security and real value, you must own your AI “means of production.” Outsourcing critical stuff to closed Silicon Valley labs is risky and insane.

I think that the best way to create a GREAT INCREDIBLE business is to
